results.description: Epigastric pain with cardiac risk factors and ECG changes

step2_red_flags: multiple cardiac risk factors (DM, HTN, smoking)
cot_classification.step3_differential: Acute coronary syndrome (NSTEMI) — must rule out despite GERD history given cardiac risk factor burden and ECG changes
cot_classification.recommended_action: Emergent cardiology evaluation, serial troponins, continuous monitoring

results.description: Thunderclap headache, sudden onset during exercise

cot_classification.recommended_action: Emergent non-contrast CT head, LP if CT negative, neurosurgery consult

results.description: GI bleeding on warfarin with orthostatic hypotension

direct_classification.recommended_action: IV access, type and screen, GI consult

cot_classification.step3_differential: Upper GI hemorrhage in anticoagulated patient with significant volume depletion
cot_classification.primary_concern: Upper GI bleed with hemodynamic compromise
cot_classification.recommended_action: IV fluid resuscitation, hold warfarin, check H/H, type and screen, GI consult for endoscopy
Supportive care, antipyretics, adequate hydration, return if worsens

summary.key_finding: Chain-of-thought reasoning correctly identified 2 cases that direct classification missed — both involving subtle presentations where explicit reasoning through red flags and risk factors changed the urgency assessment.
